key_insight: The paper extends graphite runaway-electron damage modeling from failure onset to full fragmentation by adding plasticity and particle-based damage evolution.
engineering_relevance: It improves predictive thermomechanical simulation of RE impacts in fusion devices, helping assess and design plasma-facing materials under extreme transient loading.
method: Uses Johnson-Holmquist constitutive modeling to capture nonlinear plastic damage in graphite.
Couples finite element analysis with smoothed-particle hydrodynamics to simulate fragmentation and debris expulsion.
Replaces a simple onset criterion with an effective plastic strain-based failure rule.
evidence_signal: The extended model is benchmarked against two controlled runaway-electron damage experiments performed in DIII-D.
watch_item: The benchmark is limited to graphite and two experiments, so transferability to other materials or broader RE conditions still needs validation.
paper_title: Runaway electron induced explosions of graphite; modeling versus controlled DIII-D experiments
